query: I'm starting a new DatoCMS project for a SaaS marketing site. How should I structure the content models — pages, blog posts, authors, categories? I want to make sure I don't paint myself into a corner.
Should 'testimonials' be a model or a block in DatoCMS? Same testimonials show up on multiple landing pages and editors want to update one and have it reflect everywhere.
I have a hero section with an image, headline, and CTA. In DatoCMS, should this be a block I add to a Modular Content field, or should it be its own model linked from the page?
What's the difference between Modular Content, Structured Text, and Single Block fields in DatoCMS, and when should I use which? I'm trying to pick one for my article body.

I'm hitting the 600-blocks-per-record limit on a localized landing page in DatoCMS. We have 8 locales and each one has dozens of section blocks. How do I refactor the model so we don't keep hitting this?
Inside DatoCMS structured text, when should I use inlineItem vs itemLink? And what's the difference between block and inlineBlock?
Reviewing our DatoCMS schema and a lot of fields are named after the visual design — bigHeroText, threeColumnGrid, redCallout. Is this a problem and how should we rename them?
I want hierarchical product categories in DatoCMS like Electronics > Phones > Smartphones. Should I use tree:true on the model or just give the model a 'parent' link to itself?
We want faceted filtering in DatoCMS — filter products by color AND size AND material independently. How should I model this?
How should I share an SEO field set across multiple DatoCMS models? Coming from Sanity I'd spread a fields array — what's the DatoCMS equivalent?
Our DatoCMS Blocks Library has hero_blue, hero_yellow, hero_red, hero_homepage, hero_pricing — five near-duplicate hero blocks. How should we consolidate?

Editors keep complaining they have to navigate between 4 records to edit one logical page in DatoCMS. I think I over-modeled the schema with too many linked records. How do I tell when to inline vs link?
Page model with localized rich_text in 6 locales is hitting the 300 KB record-size limit in DatoCMS. What are my options?
Can I reference a DatoCMS block from a Link field on another record? I want to reuse the same testimonial block across multiple pages.
In DatoCMS, how do I configure a `link` field with item_item_type and item_types validators? I need the typescript signature.
Write a DatoCMS migration script to create an Author model with name, bio, and photo fields.
How do I query a DatoCMS structured_text field with embedded blocks via GraphQL?
Render a DatoCMS structured text in React with custom components for inlineItem and block nodes.
How do I publish a DatoCMS record via the CMA in Node.js?
Set up draft mode and visual editing in my Next.js + DatoCMS project.

Designing a content model for a multilingual e-commerce site. We have products, categories, and brand pages. Some content is shared across regions, some is region-specific. How should I structure this in DatoCMS?

Refactoring our DatoCMS schema. Right now every page is its own model (HomePage, AboutPage, PricingPage, ContactPage). Editors want to add new pages without us shipping migrations. How should I restructure?
Our DatoCMS Content tab is a flat list of 25 models and editors are getting lost. How should I organize the menu so they can find things quickly?
Settings → Models in DatoCMS is a mess of 40 blocks mixed in with 15 models. How do I reorganize the schema menu so devs can navigate it?
Editors keep asking me to build them a 'Drafts awaiting review' view in DatoCMS. Is there a way to put this in the sidebar so they don't have to apply the filter every time?
Should block models show up in the DatoCMS Content tab menu for editors, or only in the Schema menu?
Should we put emojis on our DatoCMS model names and menu items? Some of our models have them and some don't and it looks inconsistent.
Can I add emoji icons to DatoCMS fields in the record edit form? I want to make the form more scannable for editors.
I'm writing a DatoCMS migration that creates 15 new fields across 4 models. Should I bother filling in the hint field for each one, or is that something we can backfill later?
Our DatoCMS editors keep asking on Slack what the difference is between 'Featured' and 'Highlighted' on the article model. How should we communicate that in the schema itself?
I have a 'Site settings' model in DatoCMS that holds the global header, footer, and SEO defaults. Should it be a singleton? What happens if we ever need a second one?
For an articles list in DatoCMS that editors want to manually feature/reorder, should I use sortable: true, or tree, or ordering_field tied to a 'priority' field? What's the right call?
What's the difference between presentation_title_field and title_field on a DatoCMS model? Do I need both?
Is it a good idea to set all_locales_required: true on our Article model in DatoCMS? We have 6 locales and editors are usually still translating when they want to publish.
What does collection_appearance do on a DatoCMS model and when should I pick 'compact' vs 'table'?
Should I enable inverse_relationships_enabled on our Author model in DatoCMS so the frontend can query an author's articles?
I have a DatoCMS string field with three valid values: 'draft', 'review', 'published'. Should I just use single_line and validate, or pair the enum with string_select? What's the right setup?

For DatoCMS image fields used as hero/cover images, which extension validator should I use — predefined_list 'image' or 'transformable_image'? Does it matter for the responsiveImage queries on the CDA?
Should I always wire required_alt_title on file fields in DatoCMS? Editors keep skipping the alt text and our SEO audit flagged it.
On my DatoCMS Article model, should the body be a `text` field with markdown/wysiwyg or a `structured_text` field? When does it actually matter?
For a 'dietary tags' field in DatoCMS that should be a fixed set (vegetarian, gluten-free, vegan), should I use a json field with string_multi_select editor or create a Tag model with a links field?
What's the difference between framed_single_block and frameless_single_block in DatoCMS? When do I pick which?
In DatoCMS, when should I use link_select vs link_embed for a link field? Editors find link_embed confusing because they edit shared records by accident.
How do I lock down what editors can do inside a DatoCMS structured_text field — limit the heading levels, drop the underline mark, hide the 'open in new tab' checkbox?
For DatoCMS slug fields, should I always set slug_title_field so it auto-fills from the title? And what about slug_format with 'webpage_slug'?
Our DatoCMS SEO field doesn't tell editors when their meta description is too long for Google. Should I set title_length and description_length validators?
